feedback.markdownBody:
# Review CHANGES REQUESTED for PAN-1105

# Review Synthesis — PAN-1105 — 2026-05-12T18:00:00Z

## Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED

## Context
- Manifest: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1105/.pan/review/agent-pan-1105-review-c0ae6155/context.json
- Branch: feature/1105
- Workspace: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1105

## Convoy Status
| Sub-role | Status | Blocking findings |
| --- | --- | --- |
| security | done | 0 |
| correctness | done | 0 |
| performance | done | 1 |
| requirements | done | 3 |

## Blocking Findings

### [performance] Synthesis monitor can leak an unbounded polling loop — `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:249`
The new monitor loops forever until `isPaneDeadAsync(opts.sessionId)` returns true. It does not check whether `synthesis.md` already exists until after the pane is dead, so a successful synthesis session that remains alive can leave a permanent timer and tmux poll running every 10 seconds. The cited tmux helper returns false for missing sessions because `listPaneValuesAsync()` returns an empty array on failure and `values.some(...)` is false (`src/lib/tmux.ts:603-620`), so removed sessions can also leave the loop alive indefinitely.

Fix by exiting when the synthesis file exists before polling tmux, and by bounding the monitor with a terminal condition such as review status no longer being `reviewing`, session absence, or the review timeout/deacon threshold.

### [requirements] Silent exits are detected but the required cause is not identified — Acceptance criteria
PAN-1105 requires identifying which stated failure mode caused the silent exit: context budget overflow, first-tool-call crash, wrong/unwriteable output path, or stale workspace gating. The implementation maps only generic agent state fields (`status`, `lastActivity`, `stoppedByUser`) into review notes at `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:261-273`; it does not persist or inspect the terminal/error details needed to classify those causes.

Fix by capturing the underlying agent failure signal and mapping it to the explicit accepted causes before reporting the failure.

### [requirements] Underlying errors are not surfaced in the synthesis report — Acceptance criteria
PAN-1105 requires surfacing the underlying error in the synthesis instead of the generic “exited before producing a non-empty report” failure. The changed code only calls `setReviewStatus()` with `reviewStatus: 'failed'` and `reviewNotes` at `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:280-283`; it does not write a failure `synthesis.md` artifact. Consumers expecting the synthesis report still receive no synthesis file.

Fix by writing a failure synthesis report to the expected output path, or by changing the pipeline contract so the synthesized failure artifact is explicitly read from review status with the underlying error included.

### [requirements] Required-output-path failures are not distinguished from any other missing synthesis file — Acceptance criteria
The acceptance criteria call out wrong or unwriteable required-output-file paths as a cause to identify. The implementation treats every dead synthesis session with no `synthesis.md` as the same silent failure at `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:258-283`; it does not verify the parent directory, attempted write path, permissions, or write error.

Fix by validating/capturing required-output-path write failures and reporting them separately from crashes, context-budget failures, and stale workspace gating.

## Non-blocking Findings

### [requirements]
~ The optional retry-gating criterion is directionally covered by marking review status `failed` on dead synthesis-without-output at `src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:280-283`, but the failure mode is still keyed on file absence plus pane death rather than a classified cause.

## Clean Sub-roles
- security: no findings.
- correctness: no findings.

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1105/.pan/review/agent-pan-1105-review-c0ae6155/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1105 -m "Fixed review issues"`
